subject: Computer Networking
title: Why Networks Get Attacked
description: Learn Why Networks Get Attacked in one minute.

hook.text: Every device online is a potential target. Hackers don't need a reason — they need an opening.

buildup.text: Motivations vary: money, espionage, activism, or just curiosity. Different attackers have different goals.

discovery.text: Most attacks are automated. Bots scan millions of IPs looking for known vulnerabilities. No one is specifically chosen.

twist.text: Small businesses think they're too small to target. But bots don't care about size — they care about open ports.

climax.text: The cost of cybercrime is estimated at $10 trillion annually. It's the biggest transfer of wealth in history.

punchline.text: If it's connected, it's a target. Size doesn't matter.

quiz.question: How do most network attacks begin?
options: Automated bots scanning for known vulnerabilities
